title: Are Movie Theaters Screwed?
format: Sean and Amanda on the AMC-Universal deal that cut the theatrical window to 17 days and a theater business that was already breaking, then Bradley Cooper joining PTA's next film, a scaled-back fall festival season, and Seth Rogen in An American Pickle.

segments.heading: Are movie theaters screwed?
segments.note: the AMC-Universal deal and a business that was already breaking

films.title: Trolls World Tour
films.note: The PVOD experiment that started the fight: Universal put it out to premium rental in April with theaters shut and bragged about the numbers, which set off Adam Aron at AMC.

films.title: Dolittle
films.note: On the run-through of Universal's 2020 releases. Sean's word for it: preposterous.

films.title: The Turning
films.note: A little-seen Universal horror release from January.

films.title: The Photograph
films.note: Another Universal 2020 title they'd covered on the show.

films.title: The Invisible Man
films.note: Universal's one real 2020 hit. Amanda points out its late run overlapped the first lockdown, which muddies the box-office read.

films.title: The Hunt
films.note: On the Universal 2020 slate.

films.title: The King of Staten Island
films.note: The Judd Apatow/Pete Davidson film, and the one Universal title Sean thinks might have earned a run past 17 days.

films.title: You Should Have Left
films.note: The Kevin Bacon horror movie on Universal's slate.

films.title: F9
films.note: The tentpole counterexample: makes more in theaters than at home, so it isn't going to PVOD after two and a half weeks.

films.title: Candyman
films.note: Nia DaCosta's remake of the 1992 film, produced by Jordan Peele, then dated for October 16 to land near Halloween.

films.title: The One and Only Ivan
films.note: The Disney+ gorilla movie with Bryan Cranston. Sean's example of a kids' film with no real difference between the theater and the couch.

films.title: Tenet
films.note: The Nolan release everyone's watching as the test of whether a movie can open into a reduced theater count.

films.title: The Croods: A New Age
films.note: Croods 2, which Universal kept on the calendar: a known kids property with a built-in audience, basically Trolls World Tour.

films.title: The Croods
films.note: The original, then on Netflix. Roger Deakins gave it visual-consultant help, a fact Amanda got mocked for citing on the Deakins episode.

films.title: News of the World
films.note: The Tom Hanks/Paul Greengrass awards title Universal moved off 2020.

films.title: Minions: The Rise of Gru
films.note: Delayed to 2021 (it finally landed in 2022) to hold out for a full theatrical release. Universal clearly values Despicable Me more than Croods.

films.title: Rebecca
films.note: Ben Wheatley's remake of the Hitchcock film with Armie Hammer, Lily James, and Kristin Scott Thomas. Sean's new most-anticipated, now expected at home.

segments.heading: Bradley Cooper, PTA, and Bernstein
segments.note: the week's casting news: Cooper joins Paul Thomas Anderson's untitled 1970s Valley film, then directs a Leonard Bernstein biopic

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: Cooper's directorial debut and the prestige swing they both defended. The move that keeps pushing him up the movie-star hierarchy.

films.title: The Hangover
films.note: The movie that made Cooper famous and the thing he's spent a decade trying to graduate from, studying Eastwood and Pacino to do it.

segments.heading: A scaled-back fall festival season
segments.note: Telluride, New York, and Toronto, all smaller this year

films.title: Uncut Gems
films.note: One of last year's stacked Telluride world premieres, cited to show how much slighter the 2020 slate looks.

films.title: Ford v Ferrari
films.note: Another 2019 Telluride world premiere in the comparison.

films.title: Judy
films.note: Part of last year's Telluride lineup.

films.title: First Cow
films.note: A 2019 Telluride premiere.

films.title: Marriage Story
films.note: Its Telluride North American premiere rounded out a full 2019 slate.

films.title: Ammonite
films.note: Francis Lee's Kate Winslet/Saoirse Ronan drama, on both the Telluride and TIFF lists. Neon has it, and Sean's not sure whether it goes to a window or straight home.

films.title: The Father
films.note: Florian Zeller's dementia drama with Anthony Hopkins, which Sean saw at Sundance and expects to see nominated.

films.title: Nomadland
films.note: Chloé Zhao's Frances McDormand film, getting a Telluride drive-in premiere on September 11 despite the festival being canceled.

films.title: The French Dispatch
films.note: Wes Anderson's film, pulled off the calendar entirely. The kind of title whose absence shrinks the whole season.

films.title: Mank
films.note: Netflix's Fincher film, the sort of movie a studio holds back for the biggest possible platform.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: Last year's New York Film Festival opener, set against this year opening on a TV episode.

films.title: Widows
films.note: Steve McQueen's last feature, which Amanda insists was under-discussed.

films.title: Concrete Cowboy
films.note: Among the TIFF titles announced.

films.title: Palm Springs
films.note: A Sundance movie Sean missed even after 25-plus films there.

films.title: Kajillionaire
films.note: Miranda July's new film, another Sundance title now heading to VOD in September.

films.title: She Dies Tomorrow
films.note: Amy Seimetz's film, well-timed to the apocalyptic mood, and to next week's top-five episode.

films.title: Cocoon
films.note: An actor who looked 65 for 40 straight years and never once seemed out of place.

films.title: The Thing
films.note: Always made the movie he was in better.

films.title: The Firm
films.note: The heartache line to Mitch McDeere, kept alive by Chris Ryan's impression on The Rewatchables.

segments.heading: An American Pickle
segments.note: Seth Rogen times two, on HBO Max

films.title: An American Pickle
films.note: Rogen plays a man brined for 100 years and his great-grandson. Charming and sentimental, with a hard-left third-act turn into Trump-era social media that Amanda didn't see coming but liked. Based on Simon Rich's 2013 story 'Sellout.'

films.title: Being There
films.note: The Hal Ashby comparison: a naïf everyone projects onto.

films.title: This Is the End
films.note: Sean's case for an underrated Rogen high-concept, cued up ahead of the apocalypse-movies episode.

films.title: Long Shot
films.note: The last Rogen lead they both liked.

films.title: Neighbors 2: Sorority Rising
films.note: The last time before this that Rogen carried a movie as the protagonist.

films.title: Good Boys
films.note: A big Point Grey hit, produced rather than led.

films: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery
Once Upon a Time in Hollywood

The French Dispatch.title: The French Dispatch of the Liberty, Kansas Evening Sun

Dr. Strangelove.title: Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love the Bomb

Austin Powers: International Man of Myst.title: Austin Powers: International Man of Mystery

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood
